title: centaurea vulgaris var. nigra
intro: Centaurea vulgaris var. nigra, also known as the black knapweed, is a plant species within the Asteraceae family, characterized by its dark purple flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Consuming the plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, centaurea vulgaris var. nigra is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
